text_original: Motse said: As we look back to the time when there was yet no rnlerj it seems the custom was " everybody in the world according to his own standard Accordingly each man had his own standard, ten men had ten different standards, a hundred men had a hundred different standards — the more people the more standards.
text_normalized: Motse said: As we look back to the time when there was yet no rnlerj it seems the custom was " everybody in the world according to his own standard Accordingly each man had his own standard, ten men had ten different standards, a hundred men had a hundred different standards — the more people the more standards.

text_original: And everybody approved of his own view and disapproved those of others, and so arose mutual disapproval.
text_normalized: And everybody approved of his own view and disapproved those of others, and so arose mutual disapproval.

text_original: Even father and son and brothers became enemies, since they were unable to reach any agreement.
text_normalized: Even father and son and brothers became enemies, since they were unable to reach any agreement.

text_original: Surplus energy was not employed for mutual help; excellent teachings (Tao) were kept secret; surplus goods were allowed to rot without sharing.
text_normalized: Surplus energy was not employed for mutual help; excellent teachings (Tao) were kept secret; surplus goods were allowed to rot without sharing.

text_original: The disorder in the (human) world could be compared with that among birds and beasts.
text_normalized: The disorder in the (human) world could be compared with that among birds and beasts.

text_original: The lack of regulations governing the relationships between ruler and subject, between superior and subordinate, and between elder and younger; and the absence of rules governing the relationships between father and son and between older and younger brothers, resulted in disorder in the world, Knowing the cause of the confusion to be in the absence of a ruler who could unify the standards in the world, (Heaven) chose the virtuous, sagacious, and wise in the world and crowned him emperor, charging him with the duty of unifying the wills in the empire.
text_normalized: The lack of regulations governing the relationships between ruler and subject, between superior and subordinate, and between elder and younger; and the absence of rules governing the relationships between father and son and between older and younger brothers, resulted in disorder in the world, Knowing the cause of the confusion to be in the absence of a ruler who could unify the standards in the world, (Heaven) chose the virtuous, sagacious, and wise in the world and crowned him emperor, charging him with the duty of unifying the wills in the empire.

text_original: Having been crowned, the emperor, realizing the impossibihty of unifying the world just by his own senses of hearing and sight, chose the understanding, virtuous, sagacious, and wise of the world and installed them as the three ministers, sharing with them the duty of unifying the standards in the empire.
text_normalized: Having been crowned, the emperor, realizing the impossibihty of unifying the world just by his own senses of hearing and sight, chose the understanding, virtuous, sagacious, and wise of the world and installed them as the three ministers, sharing with them the duty of unifying the standards in the empire.

text_original: The emperor and the three ministers being in office, they felt the vastness of the empire and the difficulty of unifying all the peoples in mountains and woods and those far distant.
text_normalized: The emperor and the three ministers being in office, they felt the vastness of the empire and the difficulty of unifying all the peoples in mountains and woods and those far distant.

text_original: Therefore they systematically divided up the empire, and appointed numerous feudal lords, charging these with the duty of unifying the standards in each state.
text_normalized: Therefore they systematically divided up the empire, and appointed numerous feudal lords, charging these with the duty of unifying the standards in each state.

text_original: The feudal lords in turn felt the difficulty of unifying the standards in their states just by their own senses of hearing and sight.
text_normalized: The feudal lords in turn felt the difficulty of unifying the standards in their states just by their own senses of hearing and sight.

text_original: Therefore they chose the virtuous of the state to be their ministers and secretaries and all the way down to the heads of districts and villages, sharing with them the duty of unifying the standards in the state.
text_normalized: Therefore they chose the virtuous of the state to be their ministers and secretaries and all the way down to the heads of districts and villages, sharing with them the duty of unifying the standards in the state.

text_original: When the lords of the country and the heads of the people had been appointed, the emperor issued mandates, instructing the people: ‘‘ Discovering good you must report it to your superior, discovering evil you must report it to your superior.
text_normalized: When the lords of the country and the heads of the people had been appointed, the emperor issued mandates, instructing the people: ‘‘ Discovering good you must report it to your superior, discovering evil you must report it to your superior.

text_original: What the superior thinks to be right all shall think to be right; what the superior thinks to be wrong all shall think to be wrong.
text_normalized: What the superior thinks to be right all shall think to be right; what the superior thinks to be wrong all shall think to be wrong.

text_original: When there is virtue among the people there shall be popular recommendation; when the superior is at fault there shall be good counsel.
text_normalized: When there is virtue among the people there shall be popular recommendation; when the superior is at fault there shall be good counsel.

text_original: You shall identify yourself with the superior and not associate with your subordinates.
text_normalized: You shall identify yourself with the superior and not associate with your subordinates.

text_original: So doing, one deserves encouragement from his superiors and praise from the people.
text_normalized: So doing, one deserves encouragement from his superiors and praise from the people.

text_original: On the other hand, if upon discovering good you should not report it and upon dis-covering evil you should not report it; if you should not think to be right what the superior thinks to be right, and wrong what the superior thinks to be wrong; if there should be norecomniendatioiiwhen there is virtue among the people and no good counsel when the superior is at fault; and if you should unite with the subordinates but differ from the superior— you deserve heavy punishment from your superiors and condemnation from the people/' Therefore the sage-kings of old were very Judicious and faithful in their punishments and rewards.
text_normalized: On the other hand, if upon discovering good you should not report it and upon dis-covering evil you should not report it; if you should not think to be right what the superior thinks to be right, and wrong what the superior thinks to be wrong; if there should be norecomniendatioiiwhen there is virtue among the people and no good counsel when the superior is at fault; and if you should unite with the subordinates but differ from the superior— you deserve heavy punishment from your superiors and condemnation from the people/' Therefore the sage-kings of old were very Judicious and faithful in their punishments and rewards.

text_original: And so all the people aspired to the rewards and commendation from the superior and dreaded his condemnation and punishment.
text_normalized: And so all the people aspired to the rewards and commendation from the superior and dreaded his condemnation and punishment.

text_original: Thereupon, in accord with the policy of the emperor, the village head proceeded to unify the purposes in the village.
text_normalized: Thereupon, in accord with the policy of the emperor, the village head proceeded to unify the purposes in the village.

text_original: Having accomplished this, he led the people of the village to identify themselves with the head of the district, saying: ‘'All you people of the village are to identify yourselves with the head of the district, and are not to imite with the subordinates.
text_normalized: Having accomplished this, he led the people of the village to identify themselves with the head of the district, saying: ‘'All you people of the village are to identify yourselves with the head of the district, and are not to imite with the subordinates.

text_original: What the head of the district thinks to be right all shall think to be right; what he thinks to be wrong all shall think to be wrong.
text_normalized: What the head of the district thinks to be right all shall think to be right; what he thinks to be wrong all shall think to be wrong.

text_original: Put away your evil speech and learn his good speech; put away your evil conduct and learn his good conduct.
text_normalized: Put away your evil speech and learn his good speech; put away your evil conduct and learn his good conduct.

text_original: For the head of the district is naturally the (most) virtuous of the district.
text_normalized: For the head of the district is naturally the (most) virtuous of the district.

text_original: If all the people in the district follow the example of their head, how then can the district be disorderly?
text_normalized: If all the people in the district follow the example of their head, how then can the district be disorderly?

text_original: " Now, how is it that the head of the district was so successful in governing the district 1 It was just because he could unify the purposes of the whole district that the district was so orderly.
text_normalized: " Now, how is it that the head of the district was so successful in governing the district 1 It was just because he could unify the purposes of the whole district that the district was so orderly.

text_original: Having accomplished this he in turn led the people of his district to identify themselves with the feudal lord, saying: "All you people of the district shall identify yourselves with the lord of the state, and shall not unite with the suboidinates.
text_normalized: Having accomplished this he in turn led the people of his district to identify themselves with the feudal lord, saying: "All you people of the district shall identify yourselves with the lord of the state, and shall not unite with the suboidinates.

text_original: What the lord thinks to be right all shall think to be right; what he thinks to be wrong all shall think to be wrong.
text_normalized: What the lord thinks to be right all shall think to be right; what he thinks to be wrong all shall think to be wrong.

text_original: Put away yom evil speech and learn his good speech; put away your evil conduct and learn his good conduct.
text_normalized: Put away yom evil speech and learn his good speech; put away your evil conduct and learn his good conduct.

text_original: For the lord of the state is natuxally the (most) virtuous of the state.
text_normalized: For the lord of the state is natuxally the (most) virtuous of the state.

text_original: If all the people in the state follow the example of their lord, how then can the state be in disorder?
text_normalized: If all the people in the state follow the example of their lord, how then can the state be in disorder?

text_original: Now, why was the feudal lord so successful in governing the state?
text_normalized: Now, why was the feudal lord so successful in governing the state?

text_original: It Tvas just because he could unify the purposes in the state that the state is orderly.
text_normalized: It Tvas just because he could unify the purposes in the state that the state is orderly.

text_original: Having accomplished this he in turn led the people of his state to identify them-selves with the emperor, saying: All you people of the state shall identify yourselves with the emperor and shall not unite with the subordinates.
text_normalized: Having accomplished this he in turn led the people of his state to identify them-selves with the emperor, saying: All you people of the state shall identify yourselves with the emperor and shall not unite with the subordinates.

text_original: What the emperor thinks to be right all shall think to be right; what he thinks to be wrong all shall think to be wrong.
text_normalized: What the emperor thinks to be right all shall think to be right; what he thinks to be wrong all shall think to be wrong.

text_original: Put away your evil speech and learn his good speech; put avray your evil conduct and learn his good conduct.
text_normalized: Put away your evil speech and learn his good speech; put avray your evil conduct and learn his good conduct.

text_original: For the emperor is naturally the (most) high-minded and tender-hearted man of the empire.
text_normalized: For the emperor is naturally the (most) high-minded and tender-hearted man of the empire.

text_original: If all the people of the empire follow his example, how then can the state be disorderly?
text_normalized: If all the people of the empire follow his example, how then can the state be disorderly?

text_original: Now, why was the emperor so successful in governing the empire?
text_normalized: Now, why was the emperor so successful in governing the empire?

text_original: It was just because he could unify the purposes in the empire that the empire is orderly.
text_normalized: It was just because he could unify the purposes in the empire that the empire is orderly.

text_original: But to carry the process of identification with the superior up to the Son of Heaven and not further up to Heaven itself — then the jungle from Heaven is yet unremoved.
text_normalized: But to carry the process of identification with the superior up to the Son of Heaven and not further up to Heaven itself — then the jungle from Heaven is yet unremoved.

text_original: Thereupon Heaven would send down cold and heat without moderation, and snow, frost, rain, and dew nntimelj.
text_normalized: Thereupon Heaven would send down cold and heat without moderation, and snow, frost, rain, and dew nntimelj.

text_original: As a result, the five grains could not ripen and the six animals could not mature; and there would be disease, epidemics, and pestilence.
text_normalized: As a result, the five grains could not ripen and the six animals could not mature; and there would be disease, epidemics, and pestilence.

text_original: Now the repeated visitations of hurricanes and torrents are just punishments from Heaven — punishments to the people below for not identifying themselves with it.
text_normalized: Now the repeated visitations of hurricanes and torrents are just punishments from Heaven — punishments to the people below for not identifying themselves with it.

text_original: Therefore the sage-kings of old appreciated what Heaven and the spirits desire and avoided what they abominate, in order to increase benefits and to avoid calamities in the world.
text_normalized: Therefore the sage-kings of old appreciated what Heaven and the spirits desire and avoided what they abominate, in order to increase benefits and to avoid calamities in the world.

text_original: With purification and baths and clean wine and cakes they led the people to make sacrifice and libation to Heaven and the spirits.
text_normalized: With purification and baths and clean wine and cakes they led the people to make sacrifice and libation to Heaven and the spirits.

text_original: In such services to the spirits they dared not use wine and cakes that were unclean, sacrificial animals that were not fat, or jade and silk that did not satisfy the standard requirements.
text_normalized: In such services to the spirits they dared not use wine and cakes that were unclean, sacrificial animals that were not fat, or jade and silk that did not satisfy the standard requirements.

text_original: The proper time for the spring and autumn sacrifices they dared not miss.
text_normalized: The proper time for the spring and autumn sacrifices they dared not miss.

text_original: Judging lawsuits, they dared not be unjust.
text_normalized: Judging lawsuits, they dared not be unjust.

text_original: Distributing properties, they dared not be unfair.
text_normalized: Distributing properties, they dared not be unfair.

text_original: Even when at leisure they dared not be dis-respectful.
text_normalized: Even when at leisure they dared not be dis-respectful.

text_original: When the sage-kings made such (good) rulers, Heaven and the spirits commended their leadership from above, and the people cherished it from below.
text_normalized: When the sage-kings made such (good) rulers, Heaven and the spirits commended their leadership from above, and the people cherished it from below.

text_original: To work under the hearty approval of the Heaven and the spirits is to obtain their blessings.
text_normalized: To work under the hearty approval of the Heaven and the spirits is to obtain their blessings.

text_original: To work nnder the appreciation of the people is to obtain their confidence.
text_normalized: To work nnder the appreciation of the people is to obtain their confidence.

text_original: Administering the government like this, consequently they would succeed in planning, accomplish their ends in executing, be strong in defence and victorious in attack.
text_normalized: Administering the government like this, consequently they would succeed in planning, accomplish their ends in executing, be strong in defence and victorious in attack.

text_original: And the reason for all this lies in their employing the principle of Identification with the Superior in government.
text_normalized: And the reason for all this lies in their employing the principle of Identification with the Superior in government.

text_original: And this is how the sage-kings of old administered their government.
text_normalized: And this is how the sage-kings of old administered their government.

text_original: People might then ask: At the present time rulers are not absent from the empire, why then is there disorder in the empire?
text_normalized: People might then ask: At the present time rulers are not absent from the empire, why then is there disorder in the empire?

text_original: Motse said: The political leaders of the present day are quite different from those of old.
text_normalized: Motse said: The political leaders of the present day are quite different from those of old.

text_original: The case is parallel to that of the Five Punishments with the Prince of Miao.
text_normalized: The case is parallel to that of the Five Punishments with the Prince of Miao.

text_original: In ancient times, the sage-kings made the code of the Five Punishments and put the empire in order.
text_normalized: In ancient times, the sage-kings made the code of the Five Punishments and put the empire in order.

text_original: But wfien the Prince of Miao established the Five Punishments they unsettled his empire.
text_normalized: But wfien the Prince of Miao established the Five Punishments they unsettled his empire.

text_original: Can it be that the Punishments are at fault?
text_normalized: Can it be that the Punishments are at fault?

text_original: Really the fault lies in their application.
text_normalized: Really the fault lies in their application.

text_original: The Penal Code of Lii " among the books of the ancient kings, says: Among the people of Miao punishments were applied without employing instruction and admonition.
text_normalized: The Penal Code of Lii " among the books of the ancient kings, says: Among the people of Miao punishments were applied without employing instruction and admonition.

text_original: They made a code of five tortures and called it law," This is to say, those who know how to apply punishments can govern the people with them.
text_normalized: They made a code of five tortures and called it law," This is to say, those who know how to apply punishments can govern the people with them.

text_original: And those who do not know, make five tortures out of them.
text_normalized: And those who do not know, make five tortures out of them.

text_original: Can it be that the punishments are at fault?
text_normalized: Can it be that the punishments are at fault?

text_original: Only, when their application is not to the point do they become five tortures.
text_normalized: Only, when their application is not to the point do they become five tortures.

text_original: And, also, Shu.
text_normalized: And, also, Shu.

text_original: Ling/’ among the books of the ancient kings, says: The same mouth can produce friendship or produce war." This is to say that he who can use the mouth well will produce friendship, and he who cannot will stir up the enemies and the besieging barbarians.
text_normalized: Ling/’ among the books of the ancient kings, says: The same mouth can produce friendship or produce war." This is to say that he who can use the mouth well will produce friendship, and he who cannot will stir up the enemies and the besieging barbarians.

text_original: Can it be that the mouth is at fault?
text_normalized: Can it be that the mouth is at fault?

text_original: The fault really lies in its use which stirs up the enemies and the besieging barbarians.
text_normalized: The fault really lies in its use which stirs up the enemies and the besieging barbarians.

text_original: Hence the installing of the ruler in the ancient days was intended to govern the people.
text_normalized: Hence the installing of the ruler in the ancient days was intended to govern the people.

text_original: Just as there is one thread to hold together the others in a skein and a main rope to a fishing net, so the ruler is to hold together all the evil and wicked in the empire and bring their purposes into harmony (with their superiors).
text_normalized: Just as there is one thread to hold together the others in a skein and a main rope to a fishing net, so the ruler is to hold together all the evil and wicked in the empire and bring their purposes into harmony (with their superiors).

text_original: Thus ' ‘ Hsiang Nien among the books of the ancient kings, says: " Now the empire is established and the capital is located; (Heaven) installed the emperor, kings, and lords not in order to make them proud, and (Heaven) appointed the ministers and the officials not in order to make them idle — it was to apportion duties among them and charge them with the maintenance of the Heavenly justice." This is to say that when God and the spirits in the past established the capital and installed the rulers, it was not to make their ranks high, and their emoluments substantial, and to give them wealth and honour, and let them live in comfort and free of care.
text_normalized: Thus ' ‘ Hsiang Nien among the books of the ancient kings, says: " Now the empire is established and the capital is located; (Heaven) installed the emperor, kings, and lords not in order to make them proud, and (Heaven) appointed the ministers and the officials not in order to make them idle — it was to apportion duties among them and charge them with the maintenance of the Heavenly justice." This is to say that when God and the spirits in the past established the capital and installed the rulers, it was not to make their ranks high, and their emoluments substantial, and to give them wealth and honour, and let them live in comfort and free of care.

text_original: It was really to procure benefits and eliminate adversities for the people, and to enricli the poor and increase the few, and to bring safety where there is danger and to restore order where there is confusion— it was for this that the political leaders were appointed.
text_normalized: It was really to procure benefits and eliminate adversities for the people, and to enricli the poor and increase the few, and to bring safety where there is danger and to restore order where there is confusion— it was for this that the political leaders were appointed.

text_original: And so the ancient sage-kings administered their government accordingly.
text_normalized: And so the ancient sage-kings administered their government accordingly.

text_original: The lords at the present, however, do Just the reverse.
text_normalized: The lords at the present, however, do Just the reverse.

text_original: Administration is carried on to court flattery.
text_normalized: Administration is carried on to court flattery.

text_original: Fathers and brothers and other relatives and friends are placed at the right and left and appointed rulers of the people.
text_normalized: Fathers and brothers and other relatives and friends are placed at the right and left and appointed rulers of the people.

text_original: Knowing that the superior appointed the rulers not for the welfare of the people, the people all kept aloof and would not identify themselves with the superior.
text_normalized: Knowing that the superior appointed the rulers not for the welfare of the people, the people all kept aloof and would not identify themselves with the superior.

text_original: Therefore purposes of the superior and the subordinates are not unified.
text_normalized: Therefore purposes of the superior and the subordinates are not unified.

text_original: This being so, rewards and commendations would not encourage the people to do good, and punishments and fines would not restrain them from doing evil.
text_normalized: This being so, rewards and commendations would not encourage the people to do good, and punishments and fines would not restrain them from doing evil.

text_original: How do we know this would be so?
text_normalized: How do we know this would be so?

text_original: In governing the country, the ruler proclaims; Whoever deserves reward I will reward." Suppose the purposes of the superior and the subordinates are different, whoever is rewarded by the superior would be condemned by the public.
text_normalized: In governing the country, the ruler proclaims; Whoever deserves reward I will reward." Suppose the purposes of the superior and the subordinates are different, whoever is rewarded by the superior would be condemned by the public.

text_original: And in community life the condemnation of the public is supreme.
text_normalized: And in community life the condemnation of the public is supreme.

text_original: Though there is reward from the superior, it will not be an encouragement.
text_normalized: Though there is reward from the superior, it will not be an encouragement.

text_original: In governing the country, again, the ruler proclaims: Whoever deserves punishment I will punish." Suppose the purposes of the superior and the subordinates are different, whoever is punished by the superior would be applauded by the public.
text_normalized: In governing the country, again, the ruler proclaims: Whoever deserves punishment I will punish." Suppose the purposes of the superior and the subordinates are different, whoever is punished by the superior would be applauded by the public.

text_original: And in community life the approval of the public is supreme.
text_normalized: And in community life the approval of the public is supreme.

text_original: Though there is punishment from the superior, it will not The an obstruction.
text_normalized: Though there is punishment from the superior, it will not The an obstruction.

text_original: Now, in governing the country and ruling the people, if rewards cannot encourage the people to do good and punishments cannot restrain them from doing evil, is this not just the same as in the beginning of human life when there were no rulers?
text_normalized: Now, in governing the country and ruling the people, if rewards cannot encourage the people to do good and punishments cannot restrain them from doing evil, is this not just the same as in the beginning of human life when there were no rulers?

text_original: If it is the same with rulers or without them, it is not the way to govern the people and unify the multitude.
text_normalized: If it is the same with rulers or without them, it is not the way to govern the people and unify the multitude.

text_original: As the ancient sage-kings could observe the principle of Identification with the Superior, when they became rulers the purposes of the superior and the subordinates became interchangeable.
text_normalized: As the ancient sage-kings could observe the principle of Identification with the Superior, when they became rulers the purposes of the superior and the subordinates became interchangeable.

text_original: If the superior reserved for himself special facilities the subordinates could share them.
text_normalized: If the superior reserved for himself special facilities the subordinates could share them.

text_original: If the people had any unrighted wrongs or accumulated injuries, the superior would remove them.
text_normalized: If the people had any unrighted wrongs or accumulated injuries, the superior would remove them.

text_original: Therefore, if there was a virtuous man thousands of li away, though his clansmen did not all know it (the fact) and people in the same district did not all know it, the emperor could reward him.
text_normalized: Therefore, if there was a virtuous man thousands of li away, though his clansmen did not all know it (the fact) and people in the same district did not all know it, the emperor could reward him.

text_original: And if there was an evil man thousands of li away, though his clans-men did not all know it (the fact) and people in the same village did not all know it, the emperor could punish him.
text_normalized: And if there was an evil man thousands of li away, though his clans-men did not all know it (the fact) and people in the same village did not all know it, the emperor could punish him.

text_original: Thereupon all the people in the world were astonished, and carefully avoided doing evil, saying: " The emperor is like a spirit in his hearing and sight." But the ancient kings said: It was no spirit but only the ability to make use of others’ ears and eyes to help one’s own hearing and sight, to make use of others’ lips to help one’s own speech, to make use of others’ minds to help one’s own thought, to make use of others’ limbs to help one’s own actions." When there are many to help one’s hearing and sight then of course one can hear and see far; when there are many to help one’s speech then one’s good counsel can comfort many; when there are many to help one’s thought then one’s plans can' he shaped speedily; when there are many to help one’s actions then one can accomplish one’s undertaking quickly.
text_normalized: Thereupon all the people in the world were astonished, and carefully avoided doing evil, saying: " The emperor is like a spirit in his hearing and sight." But the ancient kings said: It was no spirit but only the ability to make use of others’ ears and eyes to help one’s own hearing and sight, to make use of others’ lips to help one’s own speech, to make use of others’ minds to help one’s own thought, to make use of others’ limbs to help one’s own actions." When there are many to help one’s hearing and sight then of course one can hear and see far; when there are many to help one’s speech then one’s good counsel can comfort many; when there are many to help one’s thought then one’s plans can' he shaped speedily; when there are many to help one’s actions then one can accomplish one’s undertaking quickly.

text_original: ■ So there was no other reason for the success and great fame of the ancient sages than that they could carry out the principle of Identification with the Superior in their administration.
text_normalized: ■ So there was no other reason for the success and great fame of the ancient sages than that they could carry out the principle of Identification with the Superior in their administration.

text_original: One of the Eulogies of Chou " among the works of the ancient kings says: " They came to see His Majesty.
text_normalized: One of the Eulogies of Chou " among the works of the ancient kings says: " They came to see His Majesty.

text_original: Daily they sought the code of propriety." This describes how in times of old, the feudal lords came to the emperor’s court both spring and autumn to receive strict instructions from the emperor and ruled their states accordingly when they returned; and there was none among those who came under such an administration that dared not submit completely.
text_normalized: Daily they sought the code of propriety." This describes how in times of old, the feudal lords came to the emperor’s court both spring and autumn to receive strict instructions from the emperor and ruled their states accordingly when they returned; and there was none among those who came under such an administration that dared not submit completely.

text_original: And, at that time, none dared to confuse the instructions from the emperor.
text_normalized: And, at that time, none dared to confuse the instructions from the emperor.

text_original: And thus runs an Ode: ‘‘ My team is of white horses with black manes.
text_normalized: And thus runs an Ode: ‘‘ My team is of white horses with black manes.

text_original: The six reins look luxuriant.
text_normalized: The six reins look luxuriant.

text_original: They trot and gallop.
text_normalized: They trot and gallop.

text_original: The considerations (of the emperor) are all-inclusive." Further: My team is of dark grey horses.
text_normalized: The considerations (of the emperor) are all-inclusive." Further: My team is of dark grey horses.

text_original: The six reins look like silk.
text_normalized: The six reins look like silk.

text_original: They trot and gallop.
text_normalized: They trot and gallop.

text_original: Therefore, rewards fell upon, the.
text_normalized: Therefore, rewards fell upon, the.

text_original: virtuous and punishment upon the wicked.
text_normalized: virtuous and punishment upon the wicked.

text_original: The innocent was not prosecuted and the.guilty was not set free.
text_normalized: The innocent was not prosecuted and the.guilty was not set free.

text_original: And, all this is the result' of practising the principle of Identification with the Superior.
text_normalized: And, all this is the result' of practising the principle of Identification with the Superior.

text_original: Therefore, Motse said: Now, if the rulers and the gentle-men of the world sincerely desire to enrich their country and multiply their people, and to put the government and jurisdiction in order and stabilize the state — if so, then they cannot afford to fail to understand the principle of Identification with the Superior, which is the foundation of government, instructs an envoy set out on a mission.
text_normalized: Therefore, Motse said: Now, if the rulers and the gentle-men of the world sincerely desire to enrich their country and multiply their people, and to put the government and jurisdiction in order and stabilize the state — if so, then they cannot afford to fail to understand the principle of Identification with the Superior, which is the foundation of government, instructs an envoy set out on a mission.

text_original: The two stanzas according to iegge, Toi. iv, p.
text_normalized: The two stanzas according to iegge, Toi. iv, p.

text_original: " My horses are piebald; The six reins are like silk.
text_normalized: " My horses are piebald; The six reins are like silk.

text_original: I gallop them and urge them on, Everywhere seeking information and counsel.
text_normalized: I gallop them and urge them on, Everywhere seeking information and counsel.

text_original: My horses are white and black-maned; The six reins look glossy.
text_normalized: My horses are white and black-maned; The six reins look glossy.

text_original: I gallop them and urge them on, Everywhere seeking information and advice.’ '
text_normalized: I gallop them and urge them on, Everywhere seeking information and advice.’ '
